content:
# Signed: Assistant + gpt-4o, 2024-04-27
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
#
# Context: This is a utility function to convert arbitrary strings into URL-safe slugs, commonly used in web development for generating clean URLs from titles or user input.
#
# Confidence: 0.95 - The implementation correctly handles basic slugification needs including lowercasing, trimming, and replacing non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens.
# Open: Should this handle Unicode characters more gracefully (e.g. accented letters)? Is aggressive hyphen collapsing desired in all cases?

```python
def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
